Patch Set 1:

(2 comments)

Somewhat related to this patch, I recall a bug in the DNE code where a file being renamed from a remote directory into the local directory did not remove the entry from the REMOTE_PARENT_DIR.  Is that still a problem here, or has that been fixed?

Patch Set 1:

(3 comments)

> Somewhat related to this patch, I recall a bug in the DNE code where a file being renamed from a remote directory into the local directory did not remove the entry from the REMOTE_PARENT_DIR.  Is that still a problem here, or has that been fixed?

They are two different things: the agent object is for the name entry which object reside on remote MDT. The agent entry under REMOTE_PARENT_DIR is for the object which name entry on remote MDT. I am not sure whether the original bug is fixed or not, but there will be another patch for ZFS to handle the logic related with REMOTE_PARENT_DIR.

commit.subject: LU-10190 osd-zfs: create agent object for remote object
commit.message:
LU-10190 osd-zfs: create agent object for remote object

In DNE environment, the object and its name entry may reside
on different MDTs. Under such case, we will create an agent
object on the MDT where the name entry resides. The agent
object is empty to indicates that the real object for this
name entry resides on another MDT. If without agent object,
related name entry will be skipped when perform MDT side file
level backup/restore via ZPL by userspace tool, such as 'tar'.
